subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, V-Dem, RSF, TI, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, CPJ, ICG) + international press; Egyptian state/state-aligned media EXCLUDED there, with economic figures admitted only via IMF validation.
Structural distortion: the military's outsized, opaque role in the economy crowds out the private sector and is not fully captured in IMF headline data.

Standout drags: a debt-distress economy on IMF/Gulf life-support, no judicial independence or independent press, mass political imprisonment, and a severe water/climate tail. Offsets: a Gaza-mediator role and a young population.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), and the global energy shock. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May — the US renewed strikes on Iran ~25-26 May (Trump convened Camp David) and the war resumed, with Hormuz still blockaded. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: A debt-distress economy on external life-support, a constrained post-float currency, and a Suez earner gutted by Red Sea reroutes.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~90%+ GDP with interest consuming ~half of revenue and high financing needs, propped by an IMF program, Gulf deposits, and a 2024 megadeal; a real distress tail despite an improving primary balance. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: Post-float (March 2024) but IMF-conditioned, with a historically weak central-bank independence and rates ~21-22% to hold the pound. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The pound has no international standing, and part of reserves are Gulf deposits. (IMF)

inflation.notes: Off a ~38% peak to ~15%, still elevated and devaluation-driven. (IMF WEO 2026)

banking_stability.notes: Low reported NPLs, but an FX-shortage history and heavy sovereign-debt exposure. (World Bank; CBE via IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: The 2026 war and Red Sea/Bab-el-Mandeb shipping disruption cut Suez Canal transits and the hard-currency revenue they bring. (Reuters)

institutional.notes: IMF-validated economic data, against no judicial independence and one of the world's harshest press environments.

rule_of_law.notes: No judicial independence on political matters, with 2025 amendments expanding executive control of the judiciary. (WJP; Freedom House)

statistical_integrity.notes: Economic data is IMF-validated; political and security data are opaque. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A large bureaucracy, bloated and military-entwined, with uneven reform delivery. (IMF; BTI 2026)

press_freedom.notes: Among the world's top jailers of journalists, with critics imprisoned on 'terrorism' charges. (RSF 2026; CPJ)

political_social.notes: A young population, against entrenched military-backed rule, mass political imprisonment, and refugee-host strain.

political_stability.notes: Entrenched military-backed rule - suppressed/managed stability - against long-term pressure from the economy and a youth bulge. (Freedom House; BTI 2026)

civil_liberties.notes: Mass political imprisonment and no real political rights. (HRW; Amnesty; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Residence/golden-visa routes exist but no citizenship path, and a large refugee population faces strain and arbitrary arrests/deportations. (UNHCR; Amnesty)

social_cohesion.notes: Managed, with Coptic-minority and economic-grievance tensions. (BTI 2026)

demographics.notes: A huge young population - a dividend only if jobs exist - against a Malthusian water/jobs strain. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A courted but transactional US/Gulf partner, against an existential Nile dispute and elevated regional exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: A US/Gulf-backed partner with a pivotal Gaza-mediator role - courted but transactional. (Stimson; ICG)

conflict_involvement.notes: An existential Nile/GERD dispute, a Sinai insurgency, Red Sea spillover, and a tense Libya border. (Atlantic Council; CFR)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not sanctioned, but with recurrent FX-control and profit-repatriation friction. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: A debt-financed infrastructure build-out and a public-health win, against a severe water/climate tail.

climate.notes: An acute Nile-Delta sea-level/saline-intrusion threat plus extreme heat/water scarcity - exposure existential by 2050. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: A landmark Hepatitis-C elimination success, against an otherwise uneven and underfunded system. (WHO EMRO)

infrastructure.notes: A heavy, debt-financed build-out (a new capital, roads). (World Bank; IMF)

crime_safety.notes: Low street crime, with a terrorism-adjacent Sinai. (international advisories)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against rising climate exposure. (reinsurer data)

summary: A US/Gulf-courted regional power and pivotal Gaza mediator with a young population and a landmark Hepatitis-C public-health win - but the profile is dominated by drags: a debt-distress economy on IMF and Gulf life-support, a constrained post-float currency, a Suez Canal earner gutted by Red Sea reroutes, no judicial independence or independent press (a top jailer of journalists), mass political imprisonment, refugee-host strain with no path to belonging, and a severe water and climate tail centred on Nile dependence and a sinking Delta. A military-dominated economy crowds out the private sector throughout.
